DITA Belgian Malinois · K-9 Unit
About Dita

Born to serve.
Fearless until the end.

Dita the Hair Missile (July 2, 2014 – July 13, 2025) was a Belgian Malinois who became one of the most beloved working dogs in television history. As “Cerberus” on CBS’s SEAL Team, Dita didn’t just play the role — she performed every one of her own stunts with the precision and heart of a true operator.

Handled on and off set by Justin Melnick, a real-life police officer, their partnership was the genuine article. Dita brought authenticity to every scene, and in doing so introduced millions of viewers to the extraordinary world of military and law enforcement K-9s.

In her lifetime, Dita did whatever she could to help her fellow working dogs and their human partners. This fund is her lasting mission.

Born 2014 CBS SEAL Team Own Stunts Handler: Justin Melnick

No dog
left
behind.

The Dita Fund is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to supporting special operations K-9s and their handlers — the teams that operate in the most demanding and dangerous environments in the world.

These dogs and their human partners often lack the resources they deserve. Medical care, equipment, training, and retirement costs fall through the cracks. The Dita Fund fills those gaps.

Every dollar donated goes directly to working dogs and the handlers who trust their lives to them. This is Dita’s mission, carried forward.

Real impact.
Real teams.

501(c)3
Tax-Exempt Status
All donations are fully tax-deductible. Every contribution is transparently reported.
100%
For the Dogs & Handlers
Funds go directly to K-9 care, equipment, medical needs, and handler support programs.
11 yrs
Dita’s Legacy
A life fully lived in service. This fund is her way of continuing the fight she believed in.

Where Your Gift Goes

Four focus areas that honor Dita’s commitment to working dogs and the people who stand beside them.

01

K-9 Medical Care

Covering veterinary costs, emergency treatment, rehabilitation, and end-of-life care for special operations K-9s who’ve given everything in the line of duty.

02

Equipment & Training

Funding tactical gear, protective equipment, and specialized training resources that keep working dogs and their handlers performing at the highest level.

03

Handler Support

Supporting the human half of the team — mental health resources, transition assistance, and recognition for the handlers who dedicate their lives to this work.

04

Retirement & Adoption

Helping military and law enforcement K-9s retire with dignity — covering placement, adoption support, and ongoing care costs for dogs who’ve earned their rest.

The Handler

Justin
Melnick

Police Officer · Actor · K-9 Advocate

A working police officer who brought his genuine expertise and unbreakable bond with Dita to millions of viewers, Justin Melnick now carries her mission forward in the communities she loved. On CBS’s SEAL Team, he played Brock Reynolds — Bravo Team’s canine handler — across all seven seasons, with Dita by his side as Cerberus. Beyond SEAL Team, Justin has brought his working dogs to other productions as well, alongside Pepper the Dutchy — Dita’s sister and fellow working dog — continuing the tradition of authentic, real-world K-9 work on screen.

A life of service —
on and off the screen.

Long before the cameras rolled on SEAL Team, Justin Melnick was already living the life the show depicted. As a law enforcement officer, he understood working dogs not as props or performers, but as partners — animals whose bravery, intelligence, and loyalty are indistinguishable from that of any human officer.

When Justin brought Dita onto the set of CBS’s SEAL Team to play “Cerberus,” it wasn’t a casting decision — it was a continuation of a real partnership. Every stunt Dita performed, every scene where she demonstrated the capabilities of a working K-9, was authentic because their relationship was authentic.

But Justin’s commitment has always extended far beyond what appeared on screen. He has spent his career dedicated to his brothers and sisters in law enforcement and the military community — advocating for the men, women, and working dogs who serve in the most demanding roles imaginable, often without adequate recognition or resources.

Dita’s passing on July 13, 2025 was a profound loss. But for Justin, it also crystallized a purpose: to continue in her name exactly the work she had helped bring to light. Through The Dita Fund, he channels his dedication to the law enforcement and military communities into direct support for the K-9 teams that carry on her legacy every single day.

“She did more for working dogs than most people realize,” Justin has said. “Not just on television — but by showing the world what these animals are actually capable of, and what they give up for us. The fund is about making sure that sacrifice doesn’t go unanswered.”

Mission
Supporting special operations K-9s & handlers
in Dita’s honor
Community
Dedicated to law enforcement & the military community
on and off the set
“Dita gave everything she had, every single day — not because she was trained to, but because that’s who she was. The fund isn’t just a tribute. It’s a continuation.”
Justin Melnick  ·  Handler, Police Officer, Founder
July 2, 2014  —  July 13, 2025

For Dita.
For all of them.

In her lifetime, Dita did whatever she could to help her fellow working dogs and their human partners — through her work on television and film, and as a real working K-9. She showed the world what these animals are made of.

The Dita Fund honors her by doing the same thing she did: showing up for the working dogs and handlers who need it most.

The Dita Fund
